summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn addressed the deep spiritual and emotional exhaustion many people experience, describing it as being 'soul tired.' He began by affirming that God is our provider and source, not just a supplier, and contrasted chasing resources with chasing the Source. The sermon centered on Psalm 23:2, 'He makes me lie down in green pastures; he leads me beside quiet waters,' presenting it as a divine prescription for soul-level fatigue. Pastor Travis explained that this kind of tiredness is not fixed by physical sleep but requires spiritual rest found only in Jesus. He broke down the verse into three points: first, that God sometimes 'makes us' rest, which can come through divine disruptions like illness or waiting periods, as illustrated by the prophet Elijah's cave experience. Second, that God actively leads us, and we must follow His guidance rather than our own feelings, which can lead us astray. Third, that God leads us to 'still waters,' representing a place of internal peace and safety that replenishes our souls. The message concluded with an invitation to accept Jesus as the 'living water' and the good shepherd who provides rest, direction, and peace, encouraging those who are weary to find their replenishment in Him.
mainPoints: God prescribes rest for our souls and will sometimes 'make us' lie down in green pastures to find it, as seen in the life of Elijah.
We must allow God to lead us, not our own feelings, because only He can provide clear direction and purpose for our lives.
God leads us to 'still waters,' a place of internal peace and safety where our souls can be replenished and find calm.

versesMentioned.text: The Lord is my shepherd, I lack nothing. He makes me lie down in green pastures, he leads me beside quiet waters, he refreshes my soul. He guides me along the right paths for his name’s sake. Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they comfort me. You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies. You anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows. Surely your goodness and love will follow me all the days of my life, and I will dwell in the house of the Lord forever.
He makes me lie down in green pastures, he leads me beside quiet waters.
Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls.
There he went into a cave and spent the night. And the word of the Lord came to him: 'What are you doing here, Elijah?'
Whoever dwells in the shelter of the Most High will rest in the shadow of the Almighty. I will say of the Lord, 'He is my refuge and my fortress, my God, in whom I trust.'
There is a way that appears to be right, but in the end it leads to death.
For I know the plans I have for you,' declares the Lord, 'plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future.'
Whether you turn to the right or to the left, your ears will hear a voice behind you, saying, 'This is the way; walk in it.'
As the deer pants for streams of water, so my soul pants for you, my God.
If you declare with your mouth, 'Jesus is Lord,' and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.

Monday.scripture: Psalm 23:2 (NIV) - 'He makes me lie down in green pastures, he leads me beside quiet waters.'
Monday.focus: Recognizing your need for soul rest.
Monday.action: Identify one area where you feel 'soul tired' today and simply tell God about it in prayer.
Monday.prayer: Lord, I come to you weary. Help me to recognize my need for Your rest and to stop trying to carry this burden alone.

Tuesday.scripture: Matthew 11:28 (NIV) - 'Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.'
Tuesday.focus: Coming to Jesus as the source of rest.
Tuesday.action: Instead of turning to a distraction when tired, pause and consciously say, 'Jesus, I come to you for rest.'
Tuesday.prayer: Jesus, I choose to come to You now with my weariness. I receive the rest only You can give for my soul.

Wednesday.scripture: 1 Kings 19:9 (NIV) - 'There he went into a cave and spent the night. And the word of the Lord came to him...'
Wednesday.focus: Embracing 'cave seasons' as places for God's work.
Wednesday.action: Spend 10 minutes in a quiet place (your 'cave') without your phone, just listening for God's voice.
Wednesday.prayer: God, even in this quiet or difficult season, help me to see it as a classroom where You are teaching and restoring me.

Thursday.scripture: Proverbs 14:12 (NIV) - 'There is a way that appears to be right, but in the end it leads to death.'
Thursday.focus: Surrendering your own leadership to follow God's.
Thursday.action: Before making any decisions today, pause and ask, 'God, what is Your direction in this?'
Thursday.prayer: Shepherd, I acknowledge I cannot lead myself. Today, I choose to follow Your lead, not my own understanding.

Friday.scripture: Isaiah 30:21 (NIV) - 'Whether you turn to the right or to the left, your ears will hear a voice behind you, saying, 'This is the way; walk in it.''
Friday.focus: Listening for the Shepherd's specific guidance.
Friday.action: Write down one area where you need clarity. Listen in prayer, trusting God to speak direction for the next step, not the whole journey.
Friday.prayer: Father, quiet the noise around me. Let my ears be tuned to hear Your voice saying, 'This is the way,' for my next step.

Saturday.scripture: Psalm 42:1 (NIV) - 'As the deer pants for streams of water, so my soul pants for you, my God.'
Saturday.focus: Longing for the 'still waters' of God's presence.
Saturday.action: Find a moment of stillness today—sit quietly, take a slow walk, or listen to calming worship music—and simply be with God.
Saturday.prayer: God, my soul thirsts for You. Lead me beside Your still waters. Fill me with Your peace until my cup overflows.
